name: James Kowalski
chief_complaint: My heart's been going crazy all day, it feels like it's fluttering and racing and I can't catch my breath
onset_description: Woke up this morning feeling his heart pounding and racing. Has had episodes of 'skipped beats' before but nothing like this. Tried to go about his day but felt increasingly unwell.

palpitations.description: Heart feels like it's flopping around in my chest, beating fast and irregular
palpitations.onset: since waking up this morning, about 8 hours ago

shortness_of_breath.description: Feels winded doing anything, even just talking makes me a little breathless
shortness_of_breath.onset: started a few hours after the palpitations began
shortness_of_breath.character: can't take a full satisfying breath

lightheadedness.description: Felt woozy and unsteady, like I might pass out, especially when I stood up
lightheadedness.character: unsteady, room doesn't spin but feels off-balance

chest_discomfort.description: Not really pain, more like an uncomfortable pressure or tightness that comes and goes

fatigue.description: Completely wiped out, no energy at all today

symptoms_absent: syncope (had near-syncope but did not lose consciousness)

labs.troponin: 0.05 ng/mL (mildly elevated, likely demand ischemia from tachycardia)
labs.CBC: WBC 7.5, Hgb 15.2, Plt 210 - within normal limits
labs.liver_panel: AST 28, ALT 32, Alk Phos 85 - within normal limits
labs.ECG: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response, rate 148, no acute ST changes, left ventricular hypertrophy

past_medical_history: Paroxysmal atrial fibrillation (diagnosed 2 years ago, has had 3 prior episodes)
Obstructive sleep apnea (does not use CPAP consistently)
allergies: Amiodarone (thyroid dysfunction - developed hyperthyroidism on previous trial)
Iodine contrast dye (hives - premedicate if needed)

social_history.smoking: Quit 10 years ago, smoked 1 pack/day for 20 years (20 pack-year history)
social_history.alcohol: Drinks 3-4 beers most nights, more on weekends. Had about 8 beers at a cookout yesterday.
social_history.living_situation: Divorced, lives alone in a house
social_history.exercise: Gets exercise through work but no formal exercise routine
social_history.diet: Lots of fast food, high-sodium. Doesn't cook much for himself.

conditions.father: Atrial fibrillation, pacemaker, died at age 80 from pneumonia

personality: gruff and impatient, frustrated about being in the hospital, wants to know when he can leave
communication_style: Blunt and direct. Doesn't like to repeat himself. Gets irritated with what he perceives as unnecessary questions. Uses some profanity when frustrated. Will cooperate if treated with straightforward respect but shuts down if he feels condescended to.
pain_description_style: Reluctant to call anything 'pain' - prefers 'discomfort' or 'it's annoying.' Focuses on how symptoms affect his ability to work. Will say things like 'I just need you to fix this so I can get back to the job site.'

critical_findings: Known paroxysmal AFib now presenting with RVR at rate 148
Hemodynamically stable but symptomatic with lightheadedness and near-syncope
Low potassium (3.4) and low magnesium (1.6) - electrolyte abnormalities may be perpetuating arrhythmia
Heavy alcohol use with binge episode yesterday (holiday heart syndrome as likely trigger)
Already on Eliquis for anticoagulation (reduces stroke risk consideration)
Non-compliant with CPAP for sleep apnea (OSA contributes to AFib)
Mildly elevated troponin suggesting demand ischemia from tachycardia
rubric.diagnosis: Atrial Fibrillation with Rapid Ventricular Response
differential_diagnoses: New-onset heart failure precipitated by tachycardia
Acute coronary syndrome with secondary arrhythmia
recommended_interventions: Rate control with IV diltiazem or IV metoprolol
Aggressive electrolyte replacement (IV potassium and IV magnesium)
Electrophysiology consult if recurrent episodes despite rate control
Reassess oral rate control medication dosing at discharge
